title: senecio boelckei
intro: Senecio boelckei, also known as Boelcke's ragwort or Boelcke's groundsel, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion tribe, native to the mountainous regions of South America. The plant contains toxic compounds called pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Senecio boelckei include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol to extract its medicinal compounds.
